meta.description: Ledger of AWS GenAI Lens best practices and implementation steps that were walked but NOT promoted to a standard in the catalogue. Compiled from lens_mapping.md. Step is null when the whole BP was not promoted. focus_area is the AWS Lens question-level focus area the BP belongs to.

statuses.not_promoted: Walked and rejected via the step_promotion rubric or user direction
statuses.deferred: Substance is real but parked; not authored yet
statuses.intentionally_unmapped: Pillar or BP excluded from catalogue scope by design

not_promoted.title: Establish periodic evaluation processes
not_promoted.reason: No architecturally distinct content once vendor tooling is stripped. Scheduled running is owned by the drift monitor, model re-evaluation by the model-change gate, and owner assignment is operating-model policy, not architecture.

not_promoted.title: Perform model evaluations
not_promoted.reason: Running the evaluation is already enforced by GO1B1-01's CI gates. Tracking results over time is owned by the drift monitor.

not_promoted.title: Collect and monitor user feedback
not_promoted.reason: All four steps are vendor menus or review-process advice. The honest candidate, feedback as telemetry, adds nothing beyond an event type on the existing observability stream, and the feedback-capture UX cannot be proven by a CI gate.

not_promoted.title: Baseline performance evaluation and A/B variant testing
not_promoted.reason: Both are evaluation work already mandated by the eval-harness family of standards.

not_promoted.title: Periodic prompt review and cross-team collaboration
not_promoted.reason: Step 5 is process advice with no commitable artefact. Step 6 is a mechanical consequence of the central prompt registry; style guidelines are coaching, not a gate.

not_promoted.title: Trace consumption, dashboards, and vendor tooling steps
not_promoted.reason: Consumption advice, vendor menu items, or concerns belonging to other pillars (GENPERF, GENSEC, GENOPS02).

not_promoted.title: Automate generative AI application lifecycle with infrastructure as code
not_promoted.reason: Generic IaC and DevOps discipline owned by the base Well-Architected Framework; AWS itself cross-references five base OPS best practices. The GenAI-distinct slice (prompt registry, model catalog, eval infra as code) is already owned by existing standards.

not_promoted.title: Implement GenAIOps to optimize the application lifecycle
not_promoted.reason: Umbrella BP whose named concerns are each already owned by an existing standard. The remaining training-pipeline half is a vendor menu relevant only to teams fine-tuning their own models.

not_promoted.title: Grant least privilege access to foundation model endpoints
not_promoted.reason: Generic IAM least-privilege owned by the base WAF Security pillar (SEC02, SEC03). The approved-models slice is already enforced at the code layer by the model catalog and the no-inline-call lint.

not_promoted.title: Implement private network communication between foundation models and applications
not_promoted.reason: Pure network security (PrivateLink, VPC endpoints, private subnets) owned by the base WAF Security pillar (SEC05). Identical setup for any AWS service; no GenAI-distinct artefact.

not_promoted.title: Remaining steps of FM data-store access
not_promoted.reason: Nothing standalone survives beyond the promoted retrieval-time authorization contract.

not_promoted.title: Implement access monitoring to generative AI services and foundation models
not_promoted.reason: Generic access logging owned by the base WAF (SEC03), plus a vendor menu, plus GenAI telemetry already owned by the observability standards. End-user attribution absorbed as a recommended end_user_id field on the telemetry payload.

not_promoted.title: Implement control plane and data access monitoring
not_promoted.reason: Broad monitoring umbrella. Generic control and data plane monitoring is owned by the base WAF (SEC04); each sub-concern is already owned elsewhere (telemetry, evaluation, cost).

not_promoted.title: Implement a secure prompt catalog
not_promoted.reason: The catalog itself is already owned by the prompt-template registry standard. The only addition is generic IAM access control plus a Bedrock vendor walkthrough.

not_promoted.title: Define a ground truth data set of prompts and responses
not_promoted.reason: The same golden dataset and harness the eval-harness standards already mandate; the living-artifact requirement is the refresh standard.

not_promoted.title: Collect performance metrics from generative AI workloads
not_promoted.reason: Decomposes into telemetry, evaluation and drift, generic infrastructure observability owned by the base WAF, and vendor advice. Nothing distinct survives.

not_promoted.title: Load test model endpoints
not_promoted.reason: Generic performance engineering owned by the base WAF (PERF05) and the ML Lens. Applies only to self-hosted or provisioned serving.

not_promoted.title: Optimize inference parameters to improve response quality
not_promoted.reason: Hyperparameter tuning is technique advice. The only gateable slice, pinning inference parameters per template, is absorbed by the prompt-template manifest.

not_promoted.title: Select and customize the appropriate model for your use case
not_promoted.reason: Performance-framed twin of the model-selection record, the eval harness, and the re-evaluate-on-model-change gate. Routing, fine-tuning, and distillation are a vendor and technique menu.

not_promoted.focus_area: GENPERF03 — Optimize consumption of high-performance compute
not_promoted.title: Use managed solutions for model hosting, customization, and data access
not_promoted.reason: Vendor service-selection menu. Only bites for the minority self-hosting inference; managed-API workloads satisfy it by default with nothing to gate.

not_promoted.title: Optimize vector sizes for your use case
not_promoted.reason: Experiment-and-pick advice. The only enforceable artefact, a declared dimension with measured quality evidence, is already owned by the cost-side vector standard.

not_promoted.focus_area: GENCOST01 — Model selection and cost optimization
not_promoted.title: Identify the minimum performance requirements for a foundation model
not_promoted.reason: Decision-process advice with no commitable artefact. The requirements become content inside the model-selection record.

not_promoted.focus_area: GENCOST01 — Model selection and cost optimization
not_promoted.title: Determine the models available which meet that minimum performance bar
not_promoted.reason: Intermediate shortlist with no standalone artefact; it lives inside the model-selection record.

not_promoted.focus_area: GENCOST01 — Model selection and cost optimization
not_promoted.title: Continuously evaluate model selection
not_promoted.reason: Scored promote on the rubric but deferred from catalogue scope: auditable portfolio-scale re-evaluation needs central gateway, registry, and dashboard infrastructure beyond the catalogue's current depth.

not_promoted.title: Balance cost and performance when selecting inference paradigms (all steps)
not_promoted.reason: All four steps absorbed: the model-selection record already names hosting paradigm as a prioritized cost dimension, and its PR-coupling gate fires on hosting changes.

not_promoted.title: Demand identification, initial deployment, and demand profiling
not_promoted.reason: Process advice and observation work. The demand profile is the utilization-targets declaration inside the right-sizing baseline.

not_promoted.title: Identify a verbose prompt which could be optimized
not_promoted.reason: A template breaching its declared token ceiling is the identified verbose prompt; the footprint gate surfaces it mechanically.

not_promoted.title: Use a separate LLM to shorten the prompt
not_promoted.reason: Vendor technique suggestion. An optional authoring tool, not a gate.

not_promoted.title: Continue testing and optimizing the prompt
not_promoted.reason: Prompt-engineering technique menu and process advice; no artefact distinct from the eval-harness family.

not_promoted.title: Monitor and optimize cache usage
not_promoted.reason: Cache monitoring belongs to the observability family; cache tuning is process advice with no pre-merge artefact.

not_promoted.title: Annotate user input to enable cost-aware content filtering
not_promoted.reason: Bedrock-specific cost micro-optimisation. Once the vendor mechanism is stripped, the residual is too thin to survive as a durable, CI-gateable artefact.

not_promoted.title: Identify the smallest vector length supported by the embedding model
not_promoted.reason: A model-card property the vector standard's declaration records against; no standalone artefact.

not_promoted.title: Retrieval-quality testing and re-testing with larger vectors
not_promoted.reason: Process advice overlapping the eval harness and the performance pillar. The quality floor and result are captured in the vector standard's declaration.

not_promoted.title: Estimate the maximum time needed for an agent run
not_promoted.reason: The estimate is the declared figure the run-limits record captures; no standalone artefact.

not_promoted.title: Re-architect workflows to facilitate stopping conditions
not_promoted.reason: Tool and API timeouts are reliability error-handling belonging to a future GENREL walk. Response token limits are already covered by the per-call cap and the output budget.

not_promoted.title: Implement auto scaling and serverless architectures
not_promoted.reason: Sustainability is omitted as a top-level catalogue pillar by design; its concerns are distributed across the cost, operational excellence, and security pillars.
